text.title: java-junit-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for java-junit-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mixing `@SpringBootTest` with `@MockBean` on every test class in the suite.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each `@SpringBootTest` boots a new context; adding `@MockBean` invalidates the cache. Build time balloons from 10s to 5+ minutes. CI quota gets consumed.

failure-mode.description: Using H2 in-memory database for repository tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: H2 behavior diverges from PostgreSQL on SQL syntax, transactions, and type coercion. Tests pass with H2 and fail in prod. Always use Testcontainers with the target database.

failure-mode.description: Test names like `test1()`, `testUserService()`, or nested assertions without clear Arrange/Act/Assert.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CI logs become unreadable. Agents cannot infer what failed. Human debugging takes 3x longer.

failure-mode.description: Coverage gate set but not enforced; agents generate tests that pass the gate but fail mutation testing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mutation testing would reveal empty assertions and missing edge cases. Without it, tests look good but hide behavior changes.

failure-mode.description: Mocking both UserRepository and UserService in the same controller test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Controller logic path is entirely stubbed; the test never exercises real service code or repository contract. Controller → mocked service is correct; controller → real service → mocked repo is also correct. Never both.

failure-mode.description: Using `mockStatic(LocalDateTime.class)` without `try-with-resources`, leaving the mock active across tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Subsequent tests inherit the stubbed time. Test order dependence, flakiness, and CI intermittency result.

failure-mode.description: Calling an `@Async` method in a test without `Awaitility` — just checking results immediately after the call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Race condition. Test passes locally (single-threaded), flakes in CI under load.

failure-mode.description: Test-only `application-test.yml` with different config than production (e.g., `spring.flyway.enabled=false` in test but true in prod).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests pass in test config, features break in prod config. Catch this by running smoke tests with production config.
